description_long: This object is an industrial pumping station featuring two main cylindrical pipes, one blue and one brown, connected at a right angle and supported by robust metal brackets. The blue pipe is larger and curves upward before connecting to a rectangular metallic housing, which likely contains control or filtration components. The brown pipe runs parallel to the base and is also supported by two metal stands, ensuring stability. The entire assembly is mounted on a flat, rectangular metal base for secure installation on the ground. The surfaces of the pipes show subtle wear and rivet details, suggesting heavy-duty use in an urban infrastructure setting. The metallic housing at one end has geometric cutouts and a bolted design, indicating access for maintenance or inspection.
description: A heavy-duty urban pumping station with blue and brown metal pipes, supported by metal brackets and a rectangular control housing.
description_view_0: Side view showing both blue and brown pipes, their supporting brackets, and the rectangular metallic housing.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the hexagonal pipe openings and the face of the metallic housing.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the pipe connections and support brackets.
description_view_3: View from the end of the blue pipe, showing its circular cross-section and the base plate.
